description: This figure presents electrochemical corrosion characterization of low-density steels (Fe-30.34Mn-8.93Al-0.9C) with varying grain sizes. Panel (a) displays potentiodynamic polarization curves obtained in 3.5 wt% NaCl solution, plotting potential (V_SCE) versus current density (A/cm²) on a logarithmic scale for four samples: S0 (without solution treatment), S950 (solution treated at 950°C), S1050 (1050°C), and S1150 (1150°C). The curves reveal distinct passive behavior and breakdown characteristics across the different grain structures. Panel (b) quantifies the breakdown current density (i_bre) with error bars representing standard deviation, showing S0 exhibits the highest i_bre at 9.92 μA/cm², while the fine-grained S950 sample demonstrates the lowest i_bre of 2.72 μA/cm². Intermediate values are observed for S1050 (6.55 μA/cm²) and the coarse-grained S1150 (8.84 μA/cm²). The systematic increase in i_bre from S950 to S1150 correlates with grain coarsening, supporting the conclusion that fine grain structures promote rapid formation of protective Al-rich oxide films and superior corrosion resistance.
